summary_en: Interim dividend raised from 35 to 40 yen/share; full-year dividend revised to 85 yen/share (40 interim + 45 year-end). Change reflects upgraded FY1/27 H1 profit forecast (operating profit ¥4.1B vs. ¥3.8B prior). Prior period interim was 30 yen.
title_en: Notice regarding revision (upward) of consolidated earnings forecast for FY1/27 second quarter (interim period) and revision (dividend increase) of interim dividend forecast
translation_en_brief: On May 22, 2026, CEC (9692) issued a notice revising upward its FY1/27 interim earnings guidance and raising the interim dividend. The company lifted its consolidated operating profit forecast for the interim period (Feb 1 – Jul 31, 2026) from ¥3.8B to ¥4.1B, operating earnings from ¥3.82B to ¥4.13B, and attributable net income from ¥2.6B to ¥2.8B. The interim dividend per share was increased from 35 yen to 40 yen. The full-year dividend forecast is now 85 yen per share (40 yen interim + 45 yen year-end), versus the prior guidance of 80 yen. The prior fiscal year (FY1/26) interim dividend was 30 yen. The company cited robust demand for ICT investment driven by enterprise digital transformation and AI adoption, with major contracts awarded in the prior year and broad segment momentum supporting the upside revision. The company remains committed to enhanced shareholder returns under its long-term "VISION 2030" and medium-term plans, with the dividend adjustment reflecting both improved near-term performance and a strategic capital policy prioritizing returns to shareholders.
